text: Peak Achilles tendon loads during rehabilitation exercises vary more than 12-fold, ranging from 0.5 bodyweights (seated heel raise) to 7.3 bodyweights (forward single-leg hop).
Seated heel raises produce the lowest Achilles tendon load at approximately 0.5 bodyweights, making them an appropriate starting exercise in tendon rehabilitation progressions.
Forward single-leg hops produce the highest Achilles tendon load at approximately 7.3 bodyweights, representing an advanced end-stage rehabilitation exercise.
Achilles tendon loading can be incrementally progressed using either isolated ankle movements or multijoint movements, providing two parallel progression pathways for rehabilitation.
Asymmetric stepping movements such as lunges, step ups, and step downs allow side-specific Achilles tendon loading prescription, useful for unilateral tendon rehabilitation.
Achilles tendon rehabilitation exercise progression should account for three loading parameters: magnitude, duration, and rate of tendon loading.
